Nexus Card Executive Briefing Request Briefing
Protected Entry Point

Executive Briefing
Request

This form is the front door — not the check-in. I review every submission personally. If there's alignment, I'll reach out to schedule a discovery conversation.

There are no direct calendar links here. That's not an obstacle — it's a feature. The vetting process ensures every engagement is resourced for actual transformation.

Who This Is For

This briefing is for leaders
who are ready to be honest
about what's happening.

✓

C-suite executives facing attrition they cannot explain with standard HR language

✓

CHROs and CPOs who have tried every engagement program and need something structural

✓

CEOs and COOs who know the burnout problem is systemic but lack the diagnostic framework to prove it

✓

Conference and event organizers seeking a keynote speaker who will move the room, not just fill the hour

✓

Boards and investors reviewing organizations where talent loss is eroding mission delivery

✓

Leaders who are personally exhausted and recognize their structure is unsustainable

Who This Is Not For

This engagement is not right
for every organization.

✗

Organizations looking for a motivational speaker who will affirm rather than diagnose

✗

Leaders who need leadership to be comfortable before it can be honest

✗

Organizations unwilling to examine the structure — only the people in it

✗

Those seeking a quick workshop fix for a multi-year systemic problem

✗

Engagements where the budget decision-maker is not part of the conversation

A note on fit:

Not every organization is ready for this work — and that's not a judgment. The vetting process exists to protect both of us. If it's not the right time, I will tell you that directly. The UNMASK Blueprint Series is a strong starting point for organizations earlier in their diagnostic journey.

The Pain

You already know something is wrong.
You might not yet know what to call it.

📉

Invisible Attrition Drivers

Staff leave citing "burnout" or "better opportunities" — but the real driver is structural conditions they couldn't name. And you couldn't fix what you couldn't see.

🔁

The Replacement Spiral

You replace one burned-out leader. Within 18 months, the new one is showing the same symptoms. The structure is replicating the problem.

🔇

Silenced Capacity

Your most talented, mission-aligned people have quietly stopped speaking up. Not because they don't care — because the structure taught them their input doesn't matter.

📊

Survey Fatigue Without Change

Engagement surveys go out. Scores dip. Plans are made. Nothing structurally changes. Staff stop believing the surveys matter.

🤖

AI Amplifying the Strain

AI tools deployed without a human architecture assessment are compressing timelines and raising output expectations — without adding human capacity. The strain is accelerating.

😶‍🌫️

Leadership Running on Empty

The leaders holding everything together are the ones closest to burnout. And the organization has no structural failsafe for when they finally break.

This is what the briefing is for.

The form below gives me enough context to know whether I can help — and how. It takes 8–12 minutes. Please bring your honest assessment of what's happening.

📋

Submit This Form

Takes 8–12 minutes

🔍

Personal Review

Within 3–5 business days

📞

Discovery Call

If there's alignment

📄

Signed Agreement

Before any engagement begins

Already been approved for a briefing?

Skip the form and go straight to scheduling.

Schedule Briefing Call →
01 — Your Authority & Identity

Tell me who I'm speaking with.

02 — Organizational Context

Help me understand the structure I'd be stepping into.

03 — Problem Awareness

How clearly can you name what's happening?

04 — Investment Readiness

Transformation is not cheap. Neither is the status quo.

05 — Commitment & Alignment

The last — and most important — section.

Important: Submitting this form is not a commitment or a contract. It begins a conversation. All information shared is held in confidence. Submission does not guarantee a response if there is not a clear fit. Nikki Cates reviews every briefing personally.

Burnout is a design flaw. We fix the design.
